prompts.title: Prompt Templates
prompts.subtitle: Edit reusable workspace prompt files and duplicate templates.
prompts.empty: Create or select a prompt template.
prompts.orderSummary: Official ZeroClaw prompt files and their read order
prompts.orderSystem: Normal channel turns read workspace files from the workspace root in the source-defined bootstrap order. The file tabs below are ordered to match that read order where applicable. HEARTBEAT.md also lives at the workspace root, but is for the heartbeat worker only and is not injected into normal channel prompts. PROACTIVE.md is a zeroclaw_multi_docker optional-service convention, not an official ZeroClaw prompt file.
prompts.orderFlow:
normal channel system prompt:
  tools/safety/skills/workspace
  + AGENTS.md
  + SOUL.md
  + TOOLS.md
  + IDENTITY.md
  + USER.md
  + BOOTSTRAP.md if it exists
  + MEMORY.md when memory injection is enabled
  + current date/runtime/channel capabilities

heartbeat worker:
  reads HEARTBEAT.md from the workspace root
  parses lines that start with "- " as periodic tasks
  supports [high], [medium], [low], [paused], [completed] metadata

optional proactive service:
  may read PROACTIVE.md in this project
  gives each agent its own outbound rhythm and judgment
  not read automatically by official ZeroClaw

custom files:
  written to the workspace root only
  not read automatically unless an official file tells the agent to use them
prompts.customNote: The default contents here mirror ZeroClaw's personality template preset. Placeholders such as {agent}, {user}, {tz}, and {comm_style} are starter text that you can edit before applying a template.
prompts.officialFileHelp: This file is injected into the normal channel system prompt in the numbered order shown on its tab.
prompts.heartbeatFileHelp: This file is read by the heartbeat worker from the workspace root and is not injected into normal channel prompts.
prompts.optionalServiceFileHelp: This project convention is for the optional proactive service; official ZeroClaw will not read it automatically.
prompts.customFileHelp: This file is written to the workspace root only; reference it from an official file to make it effective.
prompts.aiFillSubtitle: Generate all files in this template using one LLM profile. Review the result, then save manually.
prompts.aiDescriptionPlaceholder: Describe who this agent is, what it does, preferences, relationship to the user, tone, boundaries, and useful context.
prompts.aiUseReferences: Send selected current files to AI as reference context

export.title: Config Export
export.subtitle: Inspect primary config, resolved config, and generated output preview.

messages.aiFillGenerated: AI generated the template draft. Review the files and click Save to persist it.
messages.invalidJsonField: {field} is not valid JSON. Please check it again.
messages.invalidNumberField: {field} must be a valid number. Please check it again.
messages.invalidMinNumberField: {field} must be greater than or equal to {min}.
messages.invalidMaxNumberField: {field} must be less than or equal to {max}.
messages.invalidUrlField: {field} must be a valid http/https URL. Please check it again.
messages.requiredField: {field} is required. Please fill it in.
messages.invalidTemplateFile: Template file names must be plain workspace file names using letters, numbers, dot, underscore, or hyphen.

confirm.deleteAgent: Delete this agent? This cannot be undone.
confirm.deleteProfile: Delete this profile? Assigned agents may need updates.

agent.id: Manager-local agent ID. Used for generated container labels, instance directories, and API routes.
agent.hostPort: Loopback port on the host that maps to this agent's ZeroClaw gateway.
agent.llmProfile: Required model provider profile. Edit model credentials and provider behavior in LLM Profiles.
agent.matrixProfile: Required Matrix profile. Matrix user, device, password, token, rooms, and behavior are controlled there.
agent.promptTemplate: Workspace prompt template to apply to this agent's instance directory.
agent.name: Optional display/container name. Leave blank to use the ID.
agent.image: ZeroClaw Docker image for this agent. Defaults to the manager's current ZeroClaw image.
agent.mcpProfile: Optional MCP gateway profile assigned to this agent.
agent.externalPeers: Required per-agent Matrix peer list used for the generated peer group and send_message_to_peer authorization. Matrix identity and credentials come from the Matrix profile.
agent.templateApplyMode: Controls how prompt template files are written when Apply template is clicked.
agent.environment: Advanced explicit environment variables merged into the generated agent container environment.
agent.proactiveEnabled: Create a per-agent proactive sidecar container that wakes this agent on its own schedule.
agent.proactiveTarget: Matrix peer target for send_message_to_peer. Leave blank to use the first External peers entry.
agent.proactiveRandomMinMinutes: Minimum random delay before the next proactive wake.
agent.proactiveRandomMaxMinutes: Maximum random delay before the next proactive wake.
agent.proactivePollSeconds: How often the sidecar checks whether the next wake time is due. Minimum is 30 seconds.
agent.proactiveQuietHours: Local quiet-hour range like 23-8. Leave blank to disable quiet hours.
agent.proactiveTimezone: Timezone used for quiet hours, for example Asia/Tokyo.
agent.proactiveChannel: Matrix channel alias passed to send_message_to_peer. Default is matrix.home.
agent.proactiveAgentUrl: Advanced full gateway URL. Leave blank to use this agent's host port and /webhook?agent=<id> automatically.
agent.proactivePrompt: Optional override for the wake prompt. Leave blank to use the built-in PROACTIVE.md-oriented prompt.

matrix.id: Reusable profile ID selected by agents.
matrix.homeserver: Matrix homeserver URL, such as https://matrix.org or your Synapse server.
matrix.userId: Bot account MXID. Required for password login and useful for identity checks.
matrix.deviceId: Stable Matrix device ID. Keep it stable for token-based E2EE sessions; password login can usually leave it blank.
matrix.password: Recommended login credential. With user ID, ZeroClaw can log in and recover corrupted Matrix state automatically.
matrix.recoveryKey: E2EE secure backup key. Lets ZeroClaw restore room keys and cross-sign the bot device on startup.
matrix.allowedRooms: Canonical room IDs accepted for inbound messages. Empty allows all rooms the bot has joined; use !room:server, not #aliases.
matrix.mentionOnly: In group rooms, respond only when the bot is mentioned. Direct messages are still processed.
matrix.replyInThread: Send replies inside Matrix threads rooted at the triggering message.
matrix.ackReactions: React while processing and when done, if enabled.
matrix.interruptOnNewMessage: Cancel an in-flight answer when a newer message arrives for the same conversation.
matrix.streamMode: off sends one final reply, partial edits a draft, multi_message splits paragraphs into separate messages.
matrix.multiMessageDelayMs: Delay between paragraph messages when stream mode is multi_message. Default is 800 ms.
matrix.channelDebounceMs: Global channel debounce delay before dispatching incoming messages. Manager default is 3000 ms.
matrix.accessToken: Alternative token-based login. Prefer user ID plus password unless you must reuse an existing token.
matrix.draftUpdateIntervalMs: Minimum edit interval for partial streaming. Native default is 1500 ms.
matrix.approvalTimeoutSecs: How long Matrix approval prompts wait before auto-denying always-ask tools.
matrix.excludedTools: Tool names to hide from the model when responding through Matrix, one per line.
matrix.replyMinIntervalSecs: Outbound pacing floor per Matrix recipient. 0 disables pacing.
matrix.replyQueueDepthMax: Maximum queued outbound replies when pacing is active. 0 uses the runtime default.
matrix.hostIp: Host IP exposed to agent containers as matrix-host; usually inherited from Docker settings.
